force-throw.description: You can hurl Simple Weapons (Melee), or Advanced Melee Weapons your size or smaller as a Standard Action, treating it as a Thrown Weapon. The Thrown Weapon deals normal weapon damage if it hits.
force-throw.notes: Throw an Empowered Simple or Advanced melee weapon as a Standard Action against a target within 6 squares.

primitive-block.name: Primitive Block
primitive-block.description: As a Reaction, you may negate a melee attack by making a successful Use the Force check. The DC of the Use the Force check is equal to the result of the attack roll you wish to negate, and you take a cumulative -5 penalty on your Use the Force checks to use this Talent for every time you have used Primitive Block since the beginning of your last turn.
primitive-block.notes: Reaction against an incoming melee attack; reaction registry handles the Use the Force roll against the incoming attack total.

cower-enemies.name: Cower Enemies
cower-enemies.description: When you use the Persuasion skill to Intimidate, you can Intimidate all targets in a 6-Square Cone (originating from your square) instead of Intimidating a single target. All other limitations to the Intimidation use of the Persuasion skill still apply.
cower-enemies.notes: Roll/announce a cone Intimidate use. Cone placement and eligible targets remain GM/player adjudicated.

force-interrogation.name: Force Interrogation
force-interrogation.description: When you deal damage to one or more creatures by using a Force Power, you can immediately make a Persuasion check as a Free Action to Intimidate a single target you damaged.
force-interrogation.notes: Immediate Free Action after damaging a creature with a Force Power.

inquisition.name: Inquisition
inquisition.description: You are particularly adept at dealing with Force-sensitive foes. You gain a +1 bonus on attack rolls and deal +1 die of damage against targets that have the Force Sensitivity feat.
inquisition.notes: Attack/damage roller applies this when the target has Force Sensitivity and the target actor is available.

unsettling-presence.name: Unsettling Presence
unsettling-presence.description:
You can spend a Force Point as a Standard Action to create an aura of unsettling discomfort around you. You make a Use the Force check when you activate this Talent and compare the check result to the Will Defense of any creature that comes within 6 squares of you.

If your Use the Force check result equals or exceeds the creature's Will Defense, that target takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls and Skill Checks while within 6 squares of you. This aura lasts for the remainder of the encounter.
unsettling-presence.notes: Spend a Force Point and roll Use the Force; selected targets that fail are flagged with the attack penalty. Range/LoS remains adjudicated.

channel-vitality.name: Channel Vitality
channel-vitality.description: You can fuel your mastery of The Force with your own vitality. As a Swift Action, you can move -1 step down the Condition Track to gain a temporary Force Point. This temporary Force Point lasts until the end of your turn, at which point it is lost if it has not been used.
channel-vitality.notes: Moves the actor one step down the Condition Track and grants one temporary Force Point that expires at end of turn.

closed-mind.name: Closed Mind
closed-mind.description: Whenever a creature uses a Mind-Affecting effect on you that targets your Will Defense, it must roll the attack roll or Skill Check twice, taking the lower result.
closed-mind.notes: Passive defensive rule; action card rolls a manual lower-of-two reminder when the automatic caller cannot enforce it.

esoteric-technique.name: Esoteric Technique
esoteric-technique.description: When you spend a Force Point to activate a Force Technique or Force Secret, you gain Bonus Hit Points equal to 10 + your Class Level until the end of the encounter.
esoteric-technique.notes: Applies bonus HP after a Force Point is spent to activate a Force Technique or Force Secret.

mystic-mastery.name: Mystic Mastery
mystic-mastery.description: Whenever you gain a level, you also gain a number of additional Force Points equal to the number of Force Talents you possess (maximum +6).
mystic-mastery.notes: Progression/reconciliation reminder; applies at level gain, capped at +6.

regimen-mastery.name: Regimen Mastery
regimen-mastery.description: You gain a +5 Force bonus on Skill Checks made to perform a Force Regimen.
regimen-mastery.notes: Force Regimen performance checks gain +5 Force bonus. Force Regimen UI remains the authority for selected regimens.

mind-probe.name: Mind Probe
mind-probe.description:
When you touch a living creature with an Intelligence score of 3 or higher, you can use The Force to probe its mind for secrets. You must be adjacent to the target, and using the Mind Probe is a Full-Round Action. If the target is unwilling, you must succeed on a Use the Force check, equaling or exceeding the target's Will Defense. This ability otherwise functions exactly as the Gather Information skill's Learn News and Rumors, Learn Secret Information, and Locate Individual applications.

Your Use the Force check must still exceed the base Gather Information skill DCs in order to retrieve the information you seek, but you need not pay anything in bribes, and you retrieve the information as a part of the Full-Round Action. Failing the Skill Check by 5 or more does not cause someone to notice that you are seeking the information.
mind-probe.notes: Roll Use the Force against Will if unwilling and compare to the normal Gather Information DC for the information sought.

perfect-telepathy.name: Perfect Telepathy
perfect-telepathy.description: You can communicate in full sentences and complete thoughts when you use the Telepathy aspect of the Use the Force skill, instead of just in basic phrases. However, the target of your Telepathy can still only communicate in basic emotions or single thoughts.

psychic-citadel.name: Psychic Citadel
psychic-citadel.description: You gain a Force bonus to your Will Defense equal to your Class Level.
psychic-citadel.notes: Defense calculator adds the actor's Force Adept/class level as a Force bonus to Will Defense.

psychic-defenses.name: Psychic Defenses
psychic-defenses.description: Whenever another creature targets you with a Force Power with the [Mind-Affecting] descriptor, it automatically takes Force damage equal to 1d6 x your Wisdom modifier (minimum x1).
psychic-defenses.notes: Rolls the automatic Force damage dice when a Mind-Affecting Force Power targets this actor.

telepathic-intruder.name: Telepathic Intruder
telepathic-intruder.description: Whenever you use a Force Power with the [Mind-Affecting] descriptor successfully against a target, until the end of your next turn you gain a +2 Force bonus on Skill Checks made to activate [Mind-Affecting] Force Powers and Force Talents against that same target.
telepathic-intruder.notes: Records the target after a successful [Mind-Affecting] Force Power; the Force executor applies +2 when that target is passed into later [Mind-Affecting] activations.
